name: CVAT
tagline: Self-hosted annotation platform for image, video, and 3D computer-vision datasets
seoTitle: CVAT - Open-Source Computer Vision Annotation Tool
metaDescription: CVAT is a free, self-hosted tool for labeling images, video, and 3D point clouds with boxes, polygons, masks, and keypoints. MIT-licensed, with a Python SDK and REST API.
overview: CVAT (Computer Vision Annotation Tool) is the free, self-hosted open-source edition of CVAT, used to build labeled datasets for computer vision. You can annotate images, video, and 3D point clouds with bounding boxes, polygons, masks, keypoints, cuboids, and tags, then export the result in formats like COCO, YOLO, and Pascal VOC.

It is aimed at research and production AI teams who want to keep their data on their own infrastructure. Because you run the whole stack with Docker Compose, no data leaves your environment, and you can connect your own ML models to auto-label and speed up the work.

As a data labeling tool, CVAT covers the manual and automated annotation step that sits between collecting raw media and training a model. It adds task management, multi-user collaboration, review workflows, and cloud-storage integration, plus a Python SDK, a CLI, and a REST API for automating pipelines.
features: Annotate images, video, and 3D point clouds with bounding boxes, polygons, masks, keypoints, cuboids, and tags
Connect your own ML models for automatic detection, segmentation, and tracking to speed up labeling
Organize datasets into projects, tasks, and jobs, assign work to annotators, and track progress
Multi-user and multi-organization support with roles, task assignments, and review workflows
Export and import in 20+ formats including COCO, YOLO, Pascal VOC, and KITTI, with S3, Azure, and Google Cloud storage integration
Automate workflows through the cvat-sdk Python package, the cvat-cli command line tool, and the REST API

gettingStarted.intro: CVAT Community is self-hosted and runs with Docker Compose. You need Docker Engine, Docker Compose, and Git installed first.

steps.heading: Clone and start the stack
steps.body: Clone the repository and launch the services with Docker Compose. Optionally set CVAT_HOST to your IP or domain before starting.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/cvat-ai/cvat
cd cvat

# Optional: set your IP or domain
# export CVAT_HOST=your-ip-or-domain

docker compose up -d

steps.heading: Create an admin account
steps.body: Create a superuser so you can log in to the web UI.

steps.heading: Sign in and start labeling
steps.body: Open http://localhost:8080 (or your CVAT_HOST) in a Chromium-based browser, log in with your superuser account, then create a project or task, upload your data, and define labels.

steps.heading: Automate with the Python SDK or CLI (optional)
steps.body: Install the SDK or CLI to script task creation, uploads, and exports instead of using the web UI.
steps.code:
pip install cvat-sdk
pip install cvat-cli

useCases: Build labeled image or video datasets for training object detection and segmentation models while keeping all data on your own servers
Run a team labeling project with assigned annotators, review workflows, and quality checks across multiple jobs
Annotate 3D point clouds with cuboids for autonomous-driving or robotics datasets
Automate dataset creation, uploads, and exports from Python or the terminal as part of an ML pipeline
